reason: publishedHR=25.28 (CI 7.81-81.81) is implausible as a hazard ratio but plausible as an odds ratio for tildrakizumab PASI90 vs placebo (rates 103/308=33% vs 3/154=2%); estimandType=null is misleading — this is an OR being mislabeled.

publishedHR=381.26 with CI [52.81, 2753.45] for brodalumab AMAGINE-1; arises from 141/222 vs 1/220 (single-event placebo arm inflates OR); not a hazard ratio. estimandType=null but value is an unstable OR.
publishedHR=63.64 correctly labeled as OR (estimandType=OR); magnitude consistent with ixekizumab UNCOVER-3 PASI90 (242/385=63% vs 5/193=2.6%); no error but flagged as extreme OR for context.

SURPASS-4 tirzepatide 15mg vs glargine HbA1c MD published ~-0.99% (ETD vs glargine at 52wk per Del Prato 2021); reported MD=-1.15 with CI [-1.26,-1.03] is more extreme than published primary estimate (~15% relative).
TRUE-NORTH ozanimod UC induction wk10 remission published rates 80/429=18.6% vs 13/216=6.0%; reported HR=3.53 is plausible as an OR (estimandType=null should be OR).

GEMINI-2 (Crohn's, Sandborn NEJM 2013) wk6 remission was 14.5% (32/220) vedolizumab vs 6.8% (10/148) placebo — extracted counts match. However reported event rate in placebo arm corresponds to vedolizumab GEMINI-2 wk6 endpoint but the named outcome 'remission' has crude OR≈2.34 not 2.54; pmid 24859203 actually refers to Sandborn UC GEMINI-1, not GEMINI-2 Crohn's. PMID mismatch likely.

RESONATE 6-year update ibrutinib vs ofatumumab PFS HR was 0.15 (Munir 2019, Am J Hematol); reported HR=0.135 with CI [0.095, 0.19] is within ~10% but CI upper bound 0.19 is notably tighter than published 0.19 (≈0.198 in some updates) — likely a magnitude reasonable but verify against final 6-yr publication.

RESONATE-2 (Burger NEJM 2015) ibrutinib vs chlorambucil PFS HR published 0.16 (95% CI 0.09-0.28) at primary; extracted HR=0.146 (CI 0.098-0.218) is from a longer follow-up but tN=136 doesn't match published n=136 ibrutinib, n=133 chlorambucil (correct). Numbers plausible.

SOURCE tezepelumab OCS-sparing trial (Wechsler Lancet Resp Med 2022) reported FAVORABLE odds ratio (1.28 favors tezepelumab for ≥50% OCS reduction is plausible but the trial actually MISSED its primary endpoint with OR=1.28 (0.69-2.39) — the extracted CI crosses 1, consistent with the published null. Acceptable but estimandType=OR correctly labeled.

WAYFINDER trial published 2024 has HR/RR=0.55 with CI [0.42, 0.71] identical to SIROCCO (id 19) — exact duplicate effect sizes across two different trials is statistically implausible and indicates copy-paste error.

Pfizer BNT162b2 C4591001 vaccine efficacy 95% corresponds to RR=0.05; extracted RR=0.05 CI [0.025, 0.10] matches Polack NEJM 2020. Crude RR from counts (8/21720)/(162/21728)=0.049. Internally consistent.

ZF2001 published VE ~75.7% (RR~0.24) for symptomatic COVID per Dai NEJM 2022; extracted RR=0.27 CI [0.23, 0.32] gives VE 73% — within range but crude rate 158/12625 vs 580/12516 = RR 0.272 matches. Acceptable. Flagged because pmid 35305290 is Dai 2022 confirming.

Same NCT02388906 (CheckMate-238) appears 3 times (ids 25, 26, 31) with different HRs: 0.65 (CI 0.51-0.83), 0.71 (0.58-0.88), 0.71 (0.58-0.88); published RFS HR was 0.65 (Weber NEJM 2017) so id 25 matches; ids 26 and 31 use HR=0.71 which is the 12mo recurrence-free RATE difference HR from later updates — inconsistent extractions for the same trial across reviews.

QuANTUM-R (Cortes Lancet Oncol 2019) quizartinib vs salvage chemo in R/R FLT3+ AML OS HR was 0.76 (95% CI 0.58-0.98) — matches. BUT NCT02668653 is QuANTUM-R; id 28 names QuANTUM-First with same NCT — QuANTUM-First is actually NCT02668653 too? No, QuANTUM-First is NCT02668653; QuANTUM-R is NCT02039726. NCT swap between trials.

QuANTUM-First (Erba Lancet 2023) quizartinib + 7+3 in ND FLT3-ITD AML OS HR was 0.78 (95% CI 0.62-0.98) — matches reported. BUT NCT02668653 is the QuANTUM-First trial; id 27 (QuANTUM-R) shares this NCT incorrectly (QuANTUM-R is NCT02039726). One of the two trials has wrong NCT.

BE-READY bimekizumab PASI90 273/349=78% vs 1/86=1.2% → extracted HR=67.27 (CI 9.55-473.83); should be OR not HR; magnitude reflects single placebo event causing unstable OR. estimandType=null should be OR.

NCT03775200 is the COMPASS COMP360 phase 2b psilocybin trial (Goodwin NEJM 2022), not a 2021 PSILO-MDD trial; pmid 33999283 is the Carhart-Harris NEJM 2021 escitalopram comparator trial which is NCT03429075. NCT-PMID mismatch.

COMP-001 (Goodwin NEJM 2022) MADRS change at wk3 was -6.6 (95% CI -10.2, -2.9) for 25mg vs 1mg — matches extracted MD=-6.6 (CI -10.2, -3.0). BUT tE=cE=cN suggests counts not applicable for MD outcome; tN=79 for active arm but cN should be 1mg arm=79, not all 233 (5+10+25mg) participants. tE=79 (all 'events') in continuous-outcome row is nonsensical.

BE READY PASI90 extracted as 318/349=91% vs 1/86=1.2% giving HR=871.9 (CI 117.5-6483); same trial id 30 has 273/349 vs 1/86 → HR=67.27. Same trial, two different event counts and OR=871.9 is absurdly extreme even for OR — likely single-event placebo + wrong active-arm n. estimandType=null misleading.

ZUMA-1 axi-cel is a single-arm CAR-T pivotal trial (Neelapu NEJM 2017) — there is no control arm and no published HR; extracted publishedHR=0.83 (CI 0.74-0.9) with cE=cN=0 implies a phantom comparator. Single-arm trials cannot generate hazard ratios.

ULTIMATE II (Steinman NEJM 2022) ublituximab ARR rate ratio was 0.51 (vs teriflunomide); extracted HR=0.41 (CI 0.27-0.61) is for ULTIMATE I or pooled, not ULTIMATE II alone (0.51). Possible study-arm swap or pooled-estimate misattribution.
MEASURE-3 secukinumab 300mg AS ASAS20 rates 50/76=66% vs 24/74=32%; RR=2.03 (CI 1.41-2.92) plausible. Internally consistent.

QUILT-3032 N-803 + BCG is a single-arm trial (no historical-control HR published); extracted RR=0.65 (CI 0.55-0.77) with cE=cN=0 is fabricated — no comparator exists. Single-arm trials cannot produce relative effect estimates without indirect comparison.

Vivitrol naltrexone XR-OUD (Lee Lancet 2018, X:BOT) — extracted publishedHR=7.0 (CI 3.0-11.0) labeled estimandType=MD but value is OR-like and CI is symmetric absolute integers around 7 (3-11), suggesting confusion between point estimate and CI bounds being SD-like. estimandType=MD but counts 90/125 vs 38/125 produce RR≈2.4 not 7.0.

REGAIN patiromer + spironolactone normokalaemia 145/145 (100%) vs 95/145 (66%) — tE=tN=145 means ALL active subjects had event which is biologically implausible for any clinical outcome; RR=1.53 not 2.21 from these counts. Likely extracted maximum-spironolactone-dose subgroup not full ITT.

ILLUMENATE-EU Stellarex DCB vs POBA primary patency at 12mo published 70.9% vs 54.7% (Steiner JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2017); the DCB was SUPERIOR (RR<1 for restenosis or RR>1 for patency). Extracted 145/196=74% vs 65/98=66% with RR=1.11 (CI 0.94-1.31) crosses null and direction is ambiguous — but published primary was statistically significant (p=0.013). Inconsistent magnitude and CI.
COMPOSE-2 naldemedine response 138/276=50% vs 94/277=34%; RR=1.47 (CI 1.18-1.83) plausible vs published responder analysis (~47% vs 34%). Consistent.

PEDAP closed-loop pediatric T1D — extracted MD=12.0 (CI 9.0-15.0); the actual published outcome (Wadwa NEJM 2023) was time-in-range difference of +12.4 percentage points which IS plausible. BUT tE=68 (all) cE=32/34 as event counts with estimandType=MD is illegal — can't have both event counts and mean difference. Schema corruption.

REACH-1 ruxolitinib aGVHD is a single-arm phase 2 (Jagasia Blood 2020); extracted RR=0.549 (CI 0.43-0.67) with cE=cN=0 is fabricated — no comparator. Single-arm responses (54.9% ORR) being repackaged as RR.

APOLLO-B patisiran ATTR-CM (Maurer NEJM 2023) — 6MWT MD vs placebo at 12 mo was +14.69 m (95% CI 0.69-28.69) favoring patisiran; extracted MD=-16.0 (CI -23.8, -8.2) has wrong sign convention and magnitude — should be POSITIVE for 6MWT improvement. Sign-flip likely.
SYNERGY-NASH tirzepatide MASH resolution rates 65/110=59% vs 22/110=20%; RR=2.95 (CI 1.97-4.43) plausible vs published Loomba NEJM 2024 (44-62% across doses vs 10% placebo).
HELIOS-B vutrisiran ATTR-CM ACM HR was 0.65 (Fontana NEJM 2024) primary composite; extracted HR=0.67 (CI 0.47-0.95) plausibly matches secondary ACM-alone HR. Consistent.

PROMISE-1 eptinezumab 100mg quarterly (Ashina Cephalalgia 2020) — extracted RR=1.51 (CI 1.24-1.84) is for 50% responder rate (favoring eptinezumab so RR>1 is correct for response) but extracted rates 129/223=58% vs 85/222=38% match published 50% MMD-responder analysis. Direction OK if outcome is response — flagged because label 'PREVENT' suggests migraine-day reduction where lower=better.
MEZAGITAMAB-ITP platelet response 36/40=90% vs 6/20=30%; RR=3.0 (CI 1.55-5.81) plausible for anti-CD38 phase 2 response endpoint.

SCB-2019 (Clover SPECTRA) VE was 67% for any-severity (Bravo Lancet 2022); extracted RR=0.44 (CI 0.32-0.62) gives VE=56%, slightly off from published 67% for moderate-severe (RR=0.16). Endpoint definition mismatch possible.

LUTONIX-AV (Trerotola JVIR 2020) showed paclitaxel DCB SUPERIOR to POBA for primary patency (HR/RR favoring DCB, i.e. RR<1 for restenosis); extracted RR=1.46 (CI 1.13-1.88) with 87/142=61% vs 60/143=42% — this is RR for PATENCY (higher=better) so RR>1 is correct directionally IF outcome=patency. Estimand definition ambiguous; flagged for clarification.

HARMONIZE sodium zirconium cyclosilicate (Kosiborod JAMA 2014) — extracted tE=175/196 (89%) vs cE=30/62 (48%) labeled MD=-0.7 (CI -0.85,-0.55); MD is in mEq/L for K reduction (~-0.7 plausible) but event counts paired with MD outcome is schema-illegal. Estimand and counts contradict.
